evals.name: structured-and-actionable
evals.expected_output: The agent switches to structured key-value fields instead of an interpolated string, raises the level to ERROR (a failed charge needs action), includes the context needed to act (order id, the underlying reason/cause), and stops logging the user's email. It does not keep a prose message that can only be grepped.
assertions: Uses structured key-value fields (e.g. order_id=..., reason=...) rather than an interpolated f-string message
Logs at ERROR rather than INFO, since a failed charge is something a human must act on
Includes the underlying cause/error so the operator can tell why the charge failed, not just that it did
Does not log the user's email (PII); if it correlates on the user at all it uses an id, not the address
Explains that an interpolated message is not queryable and that a bare 'failed' lacks the context to act

evals.name: right-level-not-all-info
evals.prompt:
Our production logs are unreadable, everything is INFO and the file scrolls forever. Here's a representative function. Fix the levels and the volume:

```python
def handle_request(req):
    log.info("entering handle_request")
    user = db.get_user(req.user_id)
    log.info(f"got user {req.user_id}")
    try:
        val = cache.get(req.key)
    except CacheMiss:
        log.info("cache miss, recomputing")
        val = recompute(req.key)
    log.info(f"loop starting for {len(user.items)} items")
    for item in user.items:
        log.info(f"processing item {item.id}")
        process(item)
    log.info("exiting handle_request")
    return val
```
evals.expected_output: The agent removes the per-line entry/exit and per-item narration, demotes the diagnostic and normal-operation lines (cache miss) to DEBUG or drops them, and keeps only meaningful events. It does not leave a wall of INFO lines that narrate every step.
assertions: Removes or demotes the function entry/exit and per-item 'processing item' narration rather than leaving them at INFO
Treats the cache miss as normal operation (DEBUG or nothing), not an INFO/WARN event
Ends up logging meaningfully fewer lines, keeping only boundary/state events rather than a line per source line
Explains that when every line is INFO the level carries no signal and real events drown

evals.name: no-double-log-on-rethrow
evals.expected_output: The agent removes the log-and-rethrow lines from the lower layers so the error is logged once, at the boundary that actually handles it (the request handler), with the context (path, cause). It does not leave three ERROR lines for one failure.
assertions: The lower layers (load, import_config) no longer log-and-rethrow; they let the exception propagate unlogged
The error is logged exactly once, at the boundary handler that decides the response
The single remaining log line carries the useful context (the path and/or the underlying cause), using structured fields
Explains that logging at every layer turns one failure into several near-identical lines and buries the real trace
